what_it_does: Defines completion criteria for a single vertical slice of an operator-cycle feature: a channel-attached, multi-card decision-sequence that renders at-bar, is cleared by an operator, and unblocks autonomous fabric work.
observations: The overnight target is deliberately narrowed to ONE complete cycle on ONE real decision, overriding earlier breadth goals.
C1 (the merge-sa keystone card) is the gating milestone and must be verified by the lead's own eyes on the live surface.
E2 (automated BLOCKED detector) is explicitly deferred; the lead will play blocked-detector manually for this slice.
fork owns the slice's heart (channel-attachment, attribution, resume) and the slice cannot close before fork's ~1am offline window.
The file distinguishes two levels of 'card': card-kinds inside one decision (presentation/explanation/choice) versus decision.steps as a sequence of many decisions.
Co-visible vs stepped rendering is treated as a resolver output, not a stored variant; merge-sa must be co-visible.
Verification requires both FUNCTION (real state change by use) and FORM (design-system-derived visual quality) on the live surface.
Drivers use ?verify=1 so verifiers cannot ghost-clear real state.
The work is organized into groups A–F with owners, priority order, and a final exit test of two full cycles.
It references commit hashes, related build docs, and specific viewports/ports for live verification.
standouts: One vertical slice overrides building the six groups in breadth.
C1 at-bar is the non-negotiable keystone; everything else inherits its engine.
Co-visible vs stepped is a computed resolver output, not a stored variant.
E2 is deferred so the slice can prove the mechanism manually first.
conventions: Uses letter-coded groups (A–F) with numbered sub-criteria.
Each criterion is split into FUNCTION and FORM.
Status labels are VERIFIED / DESIGNED / BROKEN.
Includes priority order, owner assignments, and an exit test.
References commit SHAs and related markdown specs inline.
concerns: Very dense with project-specific jargon (DNA, RHM, merge-sa, at-bar, #1b, fork) that is not defined inside the file.
Multiple 'held lightly' design branches could create ambiguity if Tim does not confirm.
Verification depends on live surface access at specific ports and viewports that may not be available to all readers.
Deferring E2 leaves the automated blocked-detector unproven in this slice.
notes: A tightly scoped, visually-gated build checklist that trades breadth for one end-to-end operator-cycle proof.
questions: What exactly does 'at-bar' mean as a measurable visual bar?
What are DNA, RHM, fork, and merge-sa in this codebase?
How is a 'channel' represented and what is its relationship to existing DecisionsInbox?
What is operator token #1b and how is it issued/validated?
What do ports :8443 and :5174 point to, and how is the live operator surface deployed?
What is the resolver's API/contract beyond 'computed allocation'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a completion-criteria checklist for one vertical slice of the operator cycle.
apparent_themes: visual verification as the real gate
intention_signals: Trying to prevent scatter by forcing all work streams to converge on one working, beautiful, cleared decision-cycle before expanding to the full type system, stack, or engine.
novelty: Treats layout mode (co-visible vs stepped) as a runtime resolver decision rather than a stored or hand-coded variant, and explicitly scopes the first proof around a manually played blocked-detector.
contribution: A concrete, vertically-scoped acceptance checklist that ties data types, rendering, clearing, attribution, and cycle engine into one verifiable operator-cycle slice.
summary_for_embedding: OPERATOR-CYCLE-CRITERIA.md is a build-completion checklist defining one vertical slice of a channel-attached, multi-card decision-sequence that renders at-bar and can be cleared to resume autonomous fabric work.
intention_for_embedding: To keep an overnight build focused on proving one end-to-end operator cycle by function and form rather than fanning out across all layers.
